item.title: Version 1.4.1
item.description:
What's new in v1.4.1

Hotfixes and UX polish on top of v1.4.0's Run Now release.

Generated AppleScripts now launch their target apps reliably

The script generator's AppleScript guidance previously allowed patterns like: ```applescript tell application "TextEdit" activate make new document -- ← fails if TextEdit was cold-started end tell ``` This works when the target app is already running but fails with "Application isn't running" when the app is cold-started — `activate` doesn't block until the app is ready, so action commands execute against a not-yet-launched process. The script generator now produces the safer pattern: ```applescript tell application "TextEdit" to activate delay 1.5 -- give the app time to finish launching tell application "TextEdit" make new document end tell ``` Existing deployed scripts will still hit the bug — re-deploy them from the SOP detail card to get the new pattern.

Dashboard sidebar polish

  • Deployed Automations now sits above Generated SOPs in the dashboard sidebar. Once you have deployments, hitting Run Now is the most frequent action — putting the deployments at the top matches the menubar dropdown layout.
  • Run Now button shows a spinner while the script runs. Previously the click registered but the button didn't change visibly, so users would click again thinking nothing happened. Now the play-circle swaps to a spinner and disables until the run finishes.
  • The "automation count" badge on each SOP row (the bolt icon + number) now uses the standard secondary text color instead of orange. It's neutral metadata, not a warning — orange was reading as "something is wrong" when it just meant "this SOP has N automation candidates."
  • The alert badge on the Deployed Automations header (the small number indicating how many deployments need attention) is now orange instead of red. Red was reading as "the app itself is broken." Orange = "needs attention" is more accurate and less alarming.
  • A failing deployment now recovers to "active" when a run succeeds. Previously a deployment that ever hit 3 consecutive failures stayed "failing" forever, even after the underlying issue was fixed and runs started succeeding again. Now any successful run clears the failing/warning state.
  • Capture Sessions list capped at the most recent 3 instead of the most recent 10. Sessions are diagnostic; they shouldn't dominate the sidebar when you're trying to see your SOPs and deployments.

Menubar Settings button now opens the full Settings window

Previously the menubar dropdown's Settings button toggled an inline mini-panel that only showed the API key field — useless after first setup. Now it opens the full Dashboard Settings window like the Dashboard button does, so you can change hourly rate, manage tools, replay onboarding, etc. without leaving the menubar.

Coming next (v1.5)

Larger UI work that needs design + research before code:
  • SOP grouping by status — separate Approved / In Review / Completed lists, with a "SOPs" entry-point in the sidebar that navigates between them.
  • Compress/expand sidebar sections — DisclosureGroup-based; let the user collapse anything they don't need to see right now.
  • Imported SOPs visibility + drift detection — surface where they live and let the system suggest edits when your captured workflow diverges from the imported original.
  • Efficiency Coach surfacing — currently buried behind a toolbar button; deserves a more visible home (sidebar pin, recurring banner when there are unread suggestions).
  • UI design benchmark study — pull patterns from top-tier macOS productivity apps (Linear, Things 3, Notion, Raycast) and propose specific Understdy improvements.

item.title: Version 1.4.0
item.description:
What's new in v1.4.0

Run Now — Understdy is now a task runner

Understdy used to generate automation scripts. As of v1.4 it also executes them — one click and your deployed automation runs against the live environment.
  • "Run Now" button on every deployed automation, both in the SOP detail card and in the Deployments sidebar. Local scripts (Python, AppleScript, shell) execute through the existing engine; cloud-service automations (Zapier, Make, n8n) POST to a webhook URL you configure once and re-use forever. Result lands as a top-right toast notification — green for success, red for failure with the error message and a "Show more" expander for the full stderr dump.
  • `understdy://run/<deployment-id>` URL scheme for triggering a run from anywhere — macOS Shortcuts, Raycast, hotkey utilities, Focus Mode rules, Siri. Get the deployment ID from the sidebar (right-click → Copy ID) and you can wire any external trigger to fire your automation.
  • Three safety rails so Run Now never surprises you. Preview shows the exact script before execution. Dry Run runs a parse-only syntax check (py_compile / osacompile / bash -n) and reports line numbers for any syntax error. First-Run Confirmation asks once before the first manual run of any specific automation, with a "Don't ask again for this automation" checkbox if you'd rather opt out per-deployment.
  • Configure Webhook sheet for Zapier/Make/n8n deployments. Paste the webhook URL the workflow gives you, click Test Connection to fire a small JSON ping (so you can confirm your Zap/Scenario/Workflow actually receives requests before wiring Run Now), Save. The button on the deployment row swaps from orange "Configure Webhook" to green "Run Now" as soon as a valid `https://` URL is in place.

Under the hood

  • Native macOS notifications — Run Now results post through `UNUserNotificationCenter`, the same system that delivers Calendar reminders and iMessage banners. Visible from anywhere (menubar Run Now, URL scheme trigger, dashboard click), persist in Notification Center for history, respect Do Not Disturb. First Run Now after install prompts for notification permission once. Dashboard window still shows the same toast inline as a bonus when it's open.
  • Immediate "Running…" feedback — clicking Run Now posts a "Running…" notification at click time, replaced by the success/failure result when the script finishes. No more 8-60 seconds of silence after clicking.
  • Run Now is non-blocking — `Process` execution runs on a detached background task instead of the main actor. No more beach ball during Run Now (was a pre-existing issue, biting hard now that Run Now is a real surface).
  • Menubar dropdown shows Deployed Automations — quick-run section above Recent SOPs, one click to fire any deployment. Status dot + filename + green play-circle. Cloud automations show an orange link icon when their webhook URL isn't set (opens dashboard to configure).
  • Menubar header shows app version — small `v1.4.0` label next to the title so you know which build is running at a glance.
  • Webhook payload carries SOP context — `deploymentId`, `sopId`, `sopTitle`, `automationDescription`, `triggeredAt` (ISO 8601), and `userHourlyRate`. Workflows can branch on which SOP triggered them or route by ROI.
  • Backward-compatible deployment storage — old deployment JSON files (no `webhookURL` field) decode cleanly. Nothing to migrate.

Coming next

  • "Regenerate with my feedback" button — your tags, notes, and "not part of SOP" / "out of order" annotations from the SOP review get fed into the next Claude pass instead of sitting there.
  • Email integration v2 — account-level privacy controls, Apple Mail + Gmail support, corporate-Workspace-safe path that doesn't trigger admin audits.
  • Annotations ingestion — SOP-level comments and per-step notes flow into the model context on the next regenerate.

item.title: Version 1.3.0
item.description:
What's new in v1.3.0

Efficiency Coach — Phase 2 upgrades

  • Cycle-aware context-switching detection. When you're oscillating between two or three apps — the Gmail ↔ Folio loop, the Claude ↔ Codex back-and-forth — Coach now names the specific cycle instead of just "you're switching a lot." Example: "You cycled Gmail ↔ Folio 5 times in 22 minutes. Complete the Gmail batch once before returning to Folio." Low-rate oscillation (2–3 min per app) now triggers this; the old rate-only rule missed it.
  • Work-in-progress detection. Flags when you're juggling 12+ active client files in a single hour with short focus per file — the pattern most tied to cycle-time inflation. "You had 16 active files in the 2–3pm hour, each averaging 2 min of focus. Finishing one client packet before starting the next can save ~20 min."
  • Fragmented-calendar detection. When today's meetings leave no 90-minute block free OR create 4+ short gaps between meetings, Coach surfaces it and suggests consolidating. Aligned with the "deep work block available" suggestion — no more same-day contradictions where Coach said both "you have a focus block" and "your day is fragmented" for the same 75-minute gap.
  • Responsiveness Mode. New toggle in Settings → Coaching for users whose role requires immediate response to clients or colleagues (like Transaction Coordinators). When on, generic "batch email" suggestions are suppressed. Coach still watches for repeated inbox re-checks — low-value vigilance that eats focus without producing responses — and can still flag those once per week. A footer note in the Coach window tells you when a suggestion was suppressed so silent behavior doesn't feel like the feature is broken.
  • Inbox-vigilance detection recognizes Gmail in Chrome. Real-world workflows where webmail lives in the browser (app name = "Google Chrome", URL = mail.google.com) are now correctly identified as email activity. Both the generic batching suggestion and the qualified Responsiveness-Mode variant now catch browser-hosted email.

Dashboard + SOP workflow

  • Pause / Resume capture directly from the Dashboard toolbar. No more trip to the menu bar when a long export is running — click Pause from the Dashboard, the wait becomes a named step in the generated SOP when you resume.
  • Mark for Review / Approve / Archive now click through correctly. Previously the button worked but the status didn't visibly change until you clicked away and back. State machine now refreshes the view on every transition.
  • Rename an SOP in the main panel and the sidebar updates immediately. No more 3 different SOPs showing the same name in the left column while the header reflects the new title.

Settings

  • Section order re-worked so the things you actually touch (Onboarding Walkthrough, Check for Updates, Beta Feedback, Coaching toggles) are at the top. Set-and-forget fields (API key, hourly rate) moved near the bottom. About stays last.
  • Version display shows the real version. Previously the About section hardcoded "1.0.0" even on v1.2.1 installs — now reads the live build number from the app bundle.
  • Auto-Detect catches more tools. Ollama installed via the macOS app (not just Homebrew) is now picked up. ChatGPT.app presence marks OpenAI API as detected. Saving your Claude API key automatically re-runs Auto-Detect so Claude API flips to "Key configured" immediately.

Fixes

  • Grant Calendar Access now actually works after a prior denial. If you previously clicked "Don't Allow" on the calendar permission prompt, clicking Grant Access again used to do nothing (macOS won't re-prompt after denial). Understdy now deep-links straight to System Settings → Privacy → Calendars so you can toggle it on, and refreshes permission status immediately when you tab back.
  • Session manifests are forward-compatible. A future Understdy version adding new event types won't silently drop your older sessions during decode.
  • Detector-key bug where a context-switching suggestion captured at 11:45 PM could get attributed to the wrong calendar day after a midnight re-analysis. Fixed.

Coming soon (v1.4)

  • "Regenerate with my feedback" button on SOPs — your tags, notes, and "not part of SOP" / "out of order" annotations get fed into the next Claude pass instead of sitting there.
  • "Run Now" button per deployed automation — Understdy already has the execution engine for Python / AppleScript / shell scripts; wiring it to a button in the Dashboard.
  • Email integration design groundwork — account-level privacy controls, Apple Mail + Gmail (with a corporate-safe path that doesn't trigger Workspace admin audits).

item.title: Version 1.2.1
item.description:
What's new in v1.2.1

  • Pause and resume capture during long external waits. When a file export, model generation, or other blocking task runs for minutes or hours while you're mid-SOP, click Pause in the menubar. Understdy stops recording whatever happens during the wait (idle, phone calls, work on other files) and records it instead as a single named step in the generated SOP — "Wait: Folio PDF export (47 minutes)". Click Resume when the wait ends.
  • Inline label field on resume. The pause card prefills the wait label from the app you were in (e.g. "Folio export") so you can just confirm or edit, not type from scratch.
  • Running pause timer in the menubar. While paused, the menubar header shows "PAUSED 12m 34s" so you always know how long you've been waiting — and that capture is intentionally paused, not broken.

Why

Heather's beta feedback: triggering a PDF export in Folio can take anywhere from 5 minutes to an hour with no progress indicator. Without a way to pause, Understdy would either record an hour of idle activity or an hour of unrelated work — both pollute the SOP. Now that wait becomes one clean step.

Fixes

  • Session manifests are forward-compatible. If a future Understdy version adds new event types, older installs now preserve those sessions instead of silently dropping them during decode.
  • `.contextReduce` day bucket keyed off session time, not analysis time. A session captured at 11:45 PM that gets re-analyzed after midnight now attributes to the calendar day it actually happened on. Minor, but prevents a dismissal on "yesterday's" card from silently suppressing today's.

Coming soon

  • Cycle-aware coaching ("you're oscillating Gmail↔Folio 5 times in 22 minutes") rolling out with v1.3.0's Phase 2 Coach update.
  • WIP-limit detection — flags when you have 12+ active client files with short dwell, which is the pattern most tied to cycle-time inflation.

item.title: Version 1.2.0
item.description:
What's new in v1.2.0

  • Efficiency Coach window. New Coach button in the Dashboard toolbar opens a view with your efficiency score, time-saved metrics, and live suggestions based on your work patterns.
  • Coach is honest about what it can see. A panel at the top of the Coach window shows which detections are currently live (sessions, SOPs, calendar access) and what unlocks with more data or permissions. No more silent empty states.
  • Calendar-aware suggestions. Once you grant calendar access, Coach can suggest meeting prep blocks and deep-work windows aligned to your actual schedule.

Fixes

  • Context-switching detection now handles all-day background captures correctly. Previously, 48 switches during a focused 20-minute stretch could be diluted across hours of idle time and never flagged. Coach now segments each session into active windows before evaluating switch rate.
  • Your accept / dismiss decisions on suggestions survive re-analysis. Prior versions could wipe your status when suggestion copy changed; now each suggestion has a stable identity under the hood.
  • Recency fix: Coach was analyzing the oldest 5 sessions instead of the newest 5 when generating suggestions and insights.

Coming soon

  • Batching suggestions tuned to how you actually work, learned from your first week of capture data.
  • A "responsiveness mode" for users whose role requires immediate response to clients or colleagues — Coach will still suggest batching for heads-down work while respecting time you spend being responsive.

item.title: Version 1.1.0
item.description:
What's New in 1.1.0

  • Editable SOP titles and step annotations with tags
  • Accept/decline automations with dollar ROI totals
  • Corrective preview walkthrough for automations
  • Time savings dashboard with visual bar
  • Word document export with annotations
  • Guided first value onboarding path
  • "Your Day" proactive dashboard
  • API cost tracking and token budgets
  • Screenshot lifecycle management
  • Team SOP library via shared folders
  • Local AI via Ollama with machine detection
  • Sparkle auto-update (future updates are automatic!)
